answer:
Sure, let me think... Oh, actually, a creative problem-solving experience that comes to mind happened at work a couple of months ago. So, I’m a marketing associate, and my team was struggling to come up with a fresh campaign for a client in the food industry. Everything we brainstormed felt pretty run-of-the-mill, and honestly, the client was expecting something that would really stand out.

So, instead of sticking with the usual digital strategies, I decided to flip the script. I suggested that we tap into nostalgia—a huge trend lately. I proposed creating an interactive online cookbook where users could add their family recipes featuring the client’s products. People could vote on their favorites, and the winners would get featured in a special-edition cookbook. The team was all in after hearing the idea because it was personal, engaging, and social-media friendly.

Thinking outside the box like this totally changed the game. Not only did we get awesome feedback from the client, but the campaign picked up way more traction than expected. It just goes to show, sometimes you need to take a step back and color outside the lines if you want to find real solutions.
title:
Describe a problem that you solved in a creative or imaginative way.
What creative trick helped you solve a problem you faced recently?
How did thinking outside the box make your problem-solving easier?
